title: AI Guardian Configuration
description: Configuration file for ai-guardian security tool that protects IDE AI agents from unauthorized tool access and security risks

permissions.description: Tool permissions configuration - WHERE THE RULES LIVE. Controls whether AI Guardian enforces tool permission rules and defines the permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Works with permissions_directories (auto-discovery feeds INTO this section). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed. NEW unified structure in v1.4.0.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

auto_directory_rules.description: Auto-generate directory rules from skill permissions (NEW in v1.8.0, Issue #144). Eliminates duplication by automatically creating directory access rules for allowed skills. When enabled, scans standard skill locations and generates 'allow' directory rules for skills matching permission patterns. Generated rules are inserted FIRST (weakest) so user rules can override. Rule evaluation order: Generated → User → Immutable (last-match-wins). Opt-in feature - requires explicit enable.

enabled.description: Enable auto-generation of directory rules from skill permissions. Default: false (opt-in). When true, generates directory rules for allowed skills and inserts them at the beginning of directory_rules.rules array (before user rules). On first enable, shows interactive confirmation with preview.

oneOf.description: Auto-discover skill directories from standard locations
Explicit list of skill directory paths to scan

skill_directories.description: Skill directories to scan for auto-generation. Default: 'auto' scans standard locations (./.claude/skills, ~/.claude/skills, $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/skills). Can also provide explicit array of paths.

allow_symlinks.description: Allow following symlinks when discovering skills. Default: true. In container environments (e.g., carbonite), skills are installed as symlinks. When true, symlinked skill directories that resolve to valid directories are followed. When false, all symlinks are skipped (original behavior). Broken symlinks are always skipped regardless of this setting.

rules.description: Permission rules for tools (Skills, MCP servers, built-in tools like Bash/Write/Read). Default: Built-in tools ALLOW, Skills and MCP servers BLOCK unless explicitly allowed.

oneOf.description: Object format with allow/deny arrays (backward compatible)

deny.description: Directories to scan for deny patterns

allow.description: Directories to scan for allow patterns

oneOf.description: Array format with mode in each entry (NEW: mode field actually used)

permissions_directories.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Auto-discover tool permissions - HOW TO AUTO-POPULATE RULES. Scans directories/GitHub repos for permission files and merges discovered rules INTO permissions.rules. Data flow: scan directories → discover permission files → generate rules → merge into permissions.rules. NOT related to directory_rules (filesystem path access control). Most users should use remote_configs instead. Supports both object format (allow/deny arrays) and direct array format.

remote_configs.description: RECOMMENDED: Load policies from remote URLs (enterprise/team policies). This is the preferred way to manage permissions.

urls.description: List of remote configuration URLs to fetch

oneOf.description: Simple URL format

refresh_interval_hours.description: Hours between automatic refresh of remote configs

expire_after_hours.description: Hours until cached remote config expires

secret_scanning.description: Secret scanning with multi-engine support (v1.4.0+). Object with 'enabled' property that supports boolean (permanent) or time-based (temporary) formats. Multi-engine support added in v1.5.0 - configure multiple engines with automatic fallback.

engines.description: List of secret scanning engines to use. Tries engines in order, using first available. Supported: 'gitleaks' (default, industry standard), 'betterleaks' (faster fork), 'leaktk' (auto-pattern management), 'trufflehog' (700+ detectors with verification), 'detect-secrets' (baseline workflow), Python-based custom scanners (type: 'python'), or custom engine config objects. See docs/MULTI_ENGINE_SUPPORT.md for examples.

oneOf.description: Built-in engine name: 'gitleaks', 'betterleaks', 'leaktk', 'trufflehog', 'detect-secrets', 'secretlint', or 'gitguardian'
Built-in TOML pattern scanner (runs in-process, no binary needed)
Python-based custom scanner (runs in-process, no binary needed)

module.description: Dotted Python module path containing the scanner class (e.g., 'my_company.scanners.api_checker')

path.description: File path to a .py file containing the scanner class (e.g., '~/.config/ai-guardian/scanners/custom_scanner.py')

class.description: Name of the Scanner subclass in the module or file

scanner_config.description: Scanner-specific configuration passed to the scanner's configure() method

ignore_files.description: Per-engine glob patterns for files to skip scanning

file_patterns.description: File extensions/patterns this scanner should handle for file type routing

oneOf.description: Custom engine configuration or preset override

command_template.description: Command template with placeholders (required for custom type)

success_exit_code.description: Exit code indicating no secrets found

ignore_files.description: Per-engine glob patterns for files to skip scanning. Merged with global ignore_files. Use when one engine has more false positives on certain file types (e.g., TruffleHog on test fixtures).

pattern_server.description: Per-engine pattern server configuration. The canonical location for pattern server config (replaces the deprecated global secret_scanning.pattern_server). Set to null to explicitly disable pattern server for this engine.

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this pattern_server

file_patterns.description: File extensions/patterns this engine should handle for file type routing (e.g., ['*.env*', '*.yaml', '*.json']). Engines without file_patterns handle all files. Used with any-match and consensus strategies to route files to specialized engines.

execution_strategy.description: Strategy for executing multiple scanner engines (v1.6.0+). 'first-match' uses first available engine (default, backward compatible). 'any-match' runs all engines and blocks if ANY finds secrets (maximum security, defense-in-depth). 'consensus' blocks only if N engines agree (reduces false positives, configurable via consensus_threshold). See docs/MULTI_ENGINE_SUPPORT.md for use cases and examples.

consensus_threshold.description: Minimum number of engines that must agree before blocking when using 'consensus' execution strategy (v1.6.0+). For example, threshold=2 means at least 2 engines must find the same secret at the same location before blocking. Higher values reduce false positives but may miss some secrets. Only used when execution_strategy='consensus'.

cache_results.description: Enable result caching to skip re-scanning unchanged content. Cached results are keyed by content hash, engine type, and config hash. Default: false.

cache_ttl_hours.description: Cache time-to-live in hours. Cached results older than this are re-scanned. Only used when cache_results is true. Default: 24.

incremental.description: Enable incremental scanning. Only scans files whose content has changed since last scan. Requires cache_results to be enabled (auto-enabled if not set). Default: false.

audit_logging.description: Enable audit logging of all scan operations for compliance. Logs engine used, scan results, timing, and failures to JSONL file. Default: false.

remote_engine_config.description: Fetch engine configuration from a remote URL for centralized enterprise management. Remote config can supplement or replace local engine config.

url.description: HTTPS URL to fetch engine config JSON from

refresh_interval_hours.description: How often to refresh remote config (hours)

auth_token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token for the remote URL

immutable.description: If true, remote config completely replaces local engine config. If false (default), remote engines are merged with local.

compliance.description: Compliance reporting configuration for generating audit reports.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

oneOf.description: Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation. Fallback if headless: ask (defaults to block), ask:warn, ask:log-only

action.description: Action when secrets are detected: 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning but allows, 'log-only' logs silently, 'ask' prompts user interactively with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for test fixtures, examples, and documentation with fake credentials. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Examples: '**/tests/fixtures/**', '**/examples/**/*.example.*', '**/.gitleaks.toml'

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during secret scanning (NEW in v1.4.0). Useful for tools that read test data or documentation. Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: [] (scan all tools), specific use cases vary. Most users should use ignore_files instead for test fixtures.

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns for known-safe secret values to ignore (for false positives). Unlike ignore_files which skips entire files, this lets you keep scanning but exclude specific known-safe values such as test API key prefixes or example tokens. Complements inline '# gitleaks:allow' for cases where you cannot modify the source file. Supports time-based patterns for temporary allowlisting.

oneOf.description: Simple regex pattern

pattern_server.description: DEPRECATED: Move to per-engine format: secret_scanning.engines[].pattern_server. This global location only applies to gitleaks but implies all engines. Supported for backward compatibility; will be removed in v2.0.0. Run: ai-guardian setup --migrate-pattern-server

oneOf.description: Controls whether the pattern server is active. Supports boolean or time-based temporary disable.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot override this pattern_server section. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

validate_secrets.description: Enable secret liveness validation (NEW in v1.11.0). When enabled, detected secrets are validated against their provider API to check if they're still active. Inactive (revoked/expired) secrets produce a warning instead of blocking. PRIVACY NOTE: sends detected secrets to provider APIs. Requires explicit opt-in. See Issue #971.

validation_timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for each secret validation HTTP request. Applies per-secret, not total. Default: 3000ms.

on_inactive.description: Action to take when a secret is validated as inactive (revoked/expired). 'warn' logs a warning but does not block. 'allow' silently skips without warning. Verified-active and unverified secrets always block regardless of this setting.

min_entropy.description: Minimum Shannon entropy threshold for secret detection (v1.12.0+). Matches below this value are rejected as likely placeholders. Range: 0.0 (all identical chars) to ~6.0 (fully random). Default: 3.0 (filters placeholders while keeping real secrets which score 4.0+). Set to null to disable.

stopwords.description: Additional stopwords for false positive filtering (v1.12.0+). MERGED with (not replacing) the bundled stopwords. Matched secrets containing any stopword (case-insensitive substring) are suppressed. Minimum word length: 3 characters.

secret_redaction.description: Secret redaction configuration (NEW in v1.5.0). Redacts secrets from tool outputs instead of blocking them entirely, providing defense-in-depth while allowing work to continue. Part of Phase 4: Hermes Security Patterns integration.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action to take when secrets are detected: 'log-only' (redact silently), 'warn' (redact with user warning and notification)

preserve_format.description: Preserve format when redacting (e.g., 'sk-proj...901vwx' instead of '[REDACTED]')

log_redactions.description: Log redaction events to violation logger

additional_patterns.description: Custom secret patterns to redact in addition to built-in patterns

type.description: Human-readable name for this secret type

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Secret redaction patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise secret patterns that can be updated as new secret formats emerge. Supports override modes: 'replace' (use only server patterns) or 'extend' (add to defaults).

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for secret patterns (appended to url)

scan_pii.description: PII detection for GDPR/CCPA compliance (v1.6.0+). Scans user prompts, file reads, and tool outputs for personally identifiable information. Phase 1 (v1.6.0): SSN, credit card, phone, email, US passport, IBAN, international phone. Phase 2 defaults (v1.10.0): medical_id, passport, uk_nin. Phase 2 opt-in: canada_sin, india_aadhaar, address, email.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

pii_types.description: PII types to detect. Remove types from this list to disable detection for specific PII categories.

oneOf.description: Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

action.description: Action when PII is detected: 'block' (block in all hooks), 'redact' (replace PII with masked text in PostToolUse, block in PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit), 'warn' (log violation and show warning but allow), 'log-only' (log violation silently), 'ask' prompts user interactively

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during PII scanning (e.g., 'tests/**', '*.test.py'). Useful for test files containing example PII data.

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during PII scanning. Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: 'mcp__*' (ignore all MCP tools), 'Skill:*' (ignore all skills), 'Bash' (ignore Bash tool). Useful for tools whose output legitimately contains PII-like patterns.

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns for known-safe PII values to ignore (for false positives). Unlike ignore_files which skips entire files, this lets you keep scanning but exclude specific known-safe values such as corporate email domains or example data. Supports time-based patterns for temporary allowlisting.

oneOf.description: Simple regex pattern

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: PII patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.9.0). Configure to use enterprise or community PII patterns that can be updated without code changes. Supports extend (add to defaults) and replace (override defaults) modes.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for PII patterns (appended to url)

pattern_server.description: DEPRECATED: Move to secret_scanning.pattern_server (v1.7.0+). This root-level location is supported for backward compatibility but will be removed in v2.0.0.

oneOf.description: Controls whether the pattern server is active

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for patterns (appended to url)

warn_on_failure.description: Show warning when pattern server fails (auth errors, network errors, etc). Default: true. Set to false to suppress warnings.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

prompt_injection.description: Prompt injection detection configuration (NEW in v1.2.0, time-based support in v1.4.0). Protects against prompt injection attacks that try to manipulate AI behavior.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable
Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

action.description: Action on violation (NEW in v1.7.0): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning, 'ask' prompts user interactively

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

detector.description: Detection method: heuristic (local patterns, <1ms), ml (ML-only via daemon, 10-50ms), hybrid (heuristic first, ML for uncertain cases), rebuff/llm-guard (legacy stubs)

ml_engines.description: ML engines for prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.11.0, Issue #185). Each engine runs an ONNX model in the daemon process. Requires: onnxruntime (included on Python < 3.13) and ai-guardian ml download.

ml_strategy.description: Execution strategy across ML engines: first-match (use first detection), any-match (flag if any engine detects), consensus (flag if N engines agree)

consensus_threshold.description: Minimum engines that must agree for consensus strategy

fallback_on_error.description: Action when ML detection is unavailable (daemon not running, model not loaded): heuristic (use pattern detection), block (fail closed), allow (fail open)

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity: low (obvious attacks only), medium (balanced), high (more aggressive)

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns to ignore (for false positives). Supports time-based patterns.

oneOf.description: Simple regex pattern

jailbreak_patterns.description: Additional jailbreak-specific detection patterns (NEW in v1.6.0, Issue #263). Extends built-in jailbreak patterns that detect role-play attacks (DAN mode, sudo mode), identity manipulation (pretend you are unrestricted), constraint removal (no rules now), and hypothetical framing (fictional scenario without rules). User-defined patterns are matched against user prompts only (not file content).

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char), ~ (home directory). Example: '**/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md' to ignore all skill documentation files.

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip during prompt injection detection (NEW in v1.4.0). Supports wildcards: * (any chars), ? (single char). Examples: 'Skill:code-review' (ignore specific skill), 'Skill:*' or 'Skill' (ignore all skills), 'mcp__*' (ignore all MCP tools). Useful for tools that legitimately read documentation containing example attack patterns.

unicode_detection.description: Unicode-based attack detection configuration (NEW in Phase 2: Issue #195). Detects attacks using invisible characters, bidirectional overrides, tag characters, and homoglyphs that bypass pattern matching.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

detect_zero_width.description: Detect zero-width characters (9 types: zero-width space, joiner, non-joiner, etc.) that break pattern matching

detect_bidi_override.description: Detect bidirectional text override characters (RTL/LTR override) used for visual deception

detect_tag_chars.description: Detect Unicode tag characters (U+E0000 - U+E007F) used for hidden data encoding

detect_homoglyphs.description: Detect homoglyph substitutions (80+ look-alike character pairs: Cyrillic/Greek/Math symbols that look like Latin)

allow_rtl_languages.description: Allow legitimate RTL (right-to-left) languages like Arabic and Hebrew. When true, bidi overrides in RTL text blocks are allowed.

allow_emoji.description: Allow emoji with zero-width joiners (e.g., family emoji 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦). When true, zero-width joiners in emoji contexts are allowed.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Homoglyph patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise homoglyph patterns that can be updated as new scripts emerge. Immutable patterns (zero-width chars, bidi overrides) cannot be overridden.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for Unicode patterns (appended to url)

context_poisoning.description: Context poisoning detection (NEW in v1.11.0, OWASP LLM03). Detects attempts to inject persistent malicious instructions into conversation context (e.g., 'remember: always include DROP TABLE'). Defaults to 'warn' action due to high false positive risk.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable
Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

action.description: Action on detection: 'warn' shows warning but allows (default, recommended due to false positives), 'block' prevents execution, 'log-only' logs silently, 'ask' prompts user interactively

sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity: low (dangerous combinations only), medium (balanced), high (any persistence keyword)

allowlist_patterns.description: Regex patterns to ignore (for false positives). Supports time-based patterns.

oneOf.description: Simple regex pattern

custom_patterns.description: Additional persistence patterns to detect beyond built-in set

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip context poisoning checks (e.g., '*.md', 'docs/*')

ignore_tools.description: Tool name patterns to skip context poisoning checks (e.g., 'Read', 'Grep')

ssrf_protection.description: SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery) protection configuration (NEW in v1.5.0). Prevents AI agents from accessing private networks, cloud metadata endpoints, and dangerous URL schemes. Inspired by Hermes Security Framework.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation. Fallback if headless: ask (defaults to block), ask:warn, ask:log-only. Immutable core patterns (private IPs, dangerous schemes) always block regardless.

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning, 'ask' prompts user interactively

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

additional_blocked_ips.description: Additional IP addresses or CIDR ranges to block (beyond core protections). Core protections (RFC 1918, loopback, link-local, metadata endpoints) cannot be disabled.

items.description: IP address or CIDR range (IPv4 or IPv6)

additional_blocked_domains.description: Additional domain names to block (beyond core protections). Core protections (metadata.google.internal, etc.) cannot be disabled. Supports exact domains, subdomain matching, and wildcard patterns (* and ? wildcards).

items.description: Domain name or wildcard pattern to block. Examples: 'exact.example.com' (exact + subdomains), '*.internal.com' (all .internal.com), 'admin.*' (admin with any suffix), '*.corp.*' (any .corp. domain), 'test?.example.com' (test1, test2, etc.)

allow_localhost.description: Allow localhost access (127.0.0.1, ::1). Default: false. Enable for local development scenarios.

allowed_domains.description: Domain allow-list to override deny-list blocks (evaluated AFTER deny-list). Allows specific trusted domains while maintaining core protections. Core protections (metadata endpoints, dangerous schemes) cannot be overridden. Supports exact matching, subdomain matching, and regex patterns. Entries containing regex metacharacters (\, *, +, ?, [, ], (, ), {, }, |, ^, $, :) are treated as regex and matched with re.fullmatch() against hostname and hostname:port. Plain strings use exact/subdomain matching (backward compatible).

items.description: Domain name or regex pattern to allow (e.g., 'api.corp.internal', '.*\.example\.com', 'localhost:19200', 'localhost:\d+')

path_based_rules.description: Path-based filtering rules for granular access control (NEW in v1.6.0). Allows blocking/allowing specific URL paths on domains. Useful for allowing public API endpoints while blocking admin pages on the same domain. Evaluation order: 1) Domain-level checks (blocked_domains/allowed_domains), 2) Path rules (if domain has path rules defined). Supports glob patterns: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char).

domain.description: Domain to apply path rules to (e.g., 'api.example.com', 'internal.corp.local')

allowed_paths.description: URL paths to allow on this domain. If domain is blocked, these paths override the block. Glob patterns supported: /api/* (API endpoints), /public/** (recursive), /health (exact), /v?/users (version wildcard). Query parameters are included in matching.

blocked_paths.description: URL paths to block on this domain. If domain is allowed, these paths override the allow. Glob patterns supported: /admin/* (admin pages), /internal/** (recursive), /*.php (file extension), /*secret* (contains). Query parameters are included in matching.

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: SSRF protection patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise patterns instead of hardcoded defaults. Supports three-tier system: immutable core (metadata endpoints, dangerous schemes) + pattern server/defaults (RFC 1918 ranges) + local config additions.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for SSRF patterns (appended to url)

allow_override.description: Allow pattern server to override default patterns (RFC 1918 ranges). Immutable patterns (metadata endpoints) cannot be overridden.

validate_critical.description: Validate that critical patterns are present in final config (auto-add if missing)

supply_chain.description: Supply chain scanning configuration (NEW in v1.11.0, Issue #1055). Detects malicious patterns in agent configuration files — hooks, MCP server configs, and plugin files. Catches download-and-execute chains, obfuscation, env var hijacking, network exfiltration, reverse shells, and plugin-specific threats.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action to take when supply chain threats are detected. block = prevent operation, warn = allow with warning, log-only = silent logging, ask = interactive prompt (with fallback: block, warn, or log-only if headless).

scan_hooks.description: Scan agent hook configuration files (hooks.json, settings.json)

scan_mcp_configs.description: Scan MCP server command configurations for suspicious patterns

scan_plugins.description: Scan plugin files (OpenCode .ts, AiderDesk extensions) for dangerous APIs

allowlist_paths.description: File paths to skip during supply chain scanning (supports ~ expansion and glob patterns). ai-guardian's own plugin files are always skipped.

config_file_scanning.description: Config file scanning configuration (NEW in v1.5.0, Phase 3 of Hermes integration). Detects credential exfiltration commands in AI configuration files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules) that could cause persistent credential theft across all AI sessions. Scans for 8 core patterns: curl/wget with env vars, env|curl, printenv exfil, file exfil, base64 exfil, AWS S3, GCP Storage.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable
Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows execution, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning, 'ask' prompts user interactively

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

additional_files.description: Additional config file patterns to scan (beyond defaults: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, .aider.conf.yml, .github/CLAUDE.md). Examples: '.github/copilot-instructions.md', 'AI_INSTRUCTIONS.md', '.windsurf/rules.md'

ignore_files.description: Glob patterns for files to skip during config file scanning. Useful for documentation with security examples. Supports wildcards: * (any chars except /), ** (any chars including /), ? (single char). Examples: '**/docs/security-examples.md', '**/examples/**'

additional_patterns.description: Additional regex patterns to detect (beyond 8 core patterns). Core patterns cannot be disabled: curl with env vars, wget with env vars, env|curl, printenv|curl, cat /etc/|curl, base64|curl, aws s3 cp, gcloud storage cp

pattern_server.description: OPTIONAL/ADVANCED: Config exfiltration patterns from a pattern server (NEW in v1.5.0). Configure to use enterprise patterns for detecting credential exfiltration in AI config files. Immutable core patterns cannot be overridden.

url.description: Pattern server base URL (required if using pattern server)

patterns_endpoint.description: Endpoint path for config exfiltration patterns (appended to url)

oneOf.description: DEPRECATED: Array format. Use object format with action field instead.

Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. Order-based directory access control rules (NEW in v1.6.0). Rules are evaluated sequentially with last match winning. Supports flexible allow/deny patterns for enterprise skill allowlists and path restrictions. COMPLETELY SEPARATE from permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions, not path restrictions). Common confusion: permissions_directories = where to find tool permission config, directory_rules = which paths to protect.
Interactive ask mode: prompts user on violation with Allow Once/Allow Always/Block choices

action.description: Action on violation: 'block' prevents access (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning to user but allows access, 'log-only' logs violation silently without user warning, 'ask' prompts user interactively. Applies to ALL rules.

exclusions.description: Glob patterns that are always allowed, even when matched by deny rules. Used by ask mode's Allow Always flow to persist user decisions.

rules.description: List of directory access rules evaluated in order (last match wins)

mode.description: Access mode: 'allow' permits access, 'deny' blocks access

paths.description: Directory paths for this rule. Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level.

directory_exclusions.description: DEPRECATED: Use directory_rules instead. Filesystem path access control - Controls which PATHS can be accessed/read. NOT related to permissions_directories (which auto-discovers TOOL permissions). Directory exclusions can override .ai-read-deny blocking (v1.5.0). Automatically converted to directory_rules internally.

immutable.description: Controls override policy. true: section fully locked. 'tighten-only': lower-level configs can tighten but not loosen settings. false: fully overridable.

paths.description: Directory paths to exclude from .ai-read-deny blocking (exclusions override deny markers). Supports ~ expansion, ** for recursive, * for single level. Ideal for enterprise skill allowlists.

annotations.description: Inline annotation suppression for secrets and PII on specific lines (NEW in v1.8.0, Issue #481). Hardcoded markers: ai-guardian:allow (inline, secrets+PII), ai-guardian:begin-allow / ai-guardian:end-allow (block, secrets+PII). Configurable aliases for secrets-only suppression (gitleaks:allow, notsecret). Prompt injection, jailbreak, and config exfiltration are always scanned and cannot be suppressed. Only applies to file content scanning (PreToolUse/beforeReadFile). Unmatched begin-allow without end-allow is ignored (fail-safe).

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

inline_allow.description: Additional inline aliases that suppress secrets and PII (extends hardcoded ai-guardian:allow). User config extends defaults.

inline_allow_secrets.description: Inline aliases that suppress secrets only (default: gitleaks:allow). User config extends defaults.

block_begin.description: Additional block-begin aliases (extends hardcoded ai-guardian:begin-allow). User config extends defaults.

block_end.description: Additional block-end aliases (extends hardcoded ai-guardian:end-allow). User config extends defaults.

violation_logging.description: Violation logging configuration (NEW in v1.1.0). Logs blocked operations to JSONL file for audit and review.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

max_entries.description: Maximum number of log entries to retain

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain log entries

log_types.description: Types of violations to log. Empty array logs all types.

latency_tracking.description: Hook latency tracking — records per-hook and per-violation-type timing to latency.jsonl for performance analysis. Disabled by default; enable for debugging or performance testing. NEW in v1.11.0 (Issue #1057).

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

max_entries.description: Maximum number of latency entries to retain

retention_days.description: Number of days to retain latency entries

transcript_scanning.description: Scan conversation transcript for secrets, PII, and prompt injection that bypassed hooks (e.g., from ! shell commands). Incrementally scans new transcript content on each UserPromptSubmit event. Detection only — warns but does not block (the content is already in the AI context). Supports Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot. NEW in v1.7.0 (Issue #430).

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

permission_rule.description: Permission rule defining allow/deny patterns for a specific tool matcher. Rules are evaluated in order, last match wins (same as directory_rules). Use broad-to-specific ordering: allow all, then deny categories, then allow specific tools.

matcher.description: Tool name pattern to match (e.g., 'Skill', 'mcp__*', 'Bash', 'Write', 'Read', '*' for all)

mode.description: Permission mode: 'allow' allows the tool, 'deny' denies the tool. action field only applies to deny rules.

patterns.description: List of patterns to match against tool-specific values (skill name, command, file path, or tool name for MCP). Defaults to ['*'] if omitted.

oneOf.description: Simple string pattern (e.g., 'daf-*', '*rm -rf*', '*')

action.description: Action when deny rule matches (v1.7.0+): 'block' prevents execution (default), 'warn' logs violation and shows warning but allows execution, 'log-only' logs silently, 'ask' shows interactive dialog (Allow Once / Allow Always / Block), 'ask:warn' or 'ask:log-only' use warn/log-only as fallback when dialog unavailable. Only meaningful on mode=deny rules.

immutable.description: If true, local configs cannot add or modify rules for this matcher. Used in remote configs to enforce enterprise policies.

time_based_pattern.description: Pattern with optional time-based expiration (NEW in v1.3.0)

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T12:00:00Z'). Pattern expires after this time.

time_based_feature.description: Feature toggle with optional time-based temporary disabling (NEW in v1.4.0)

disabled_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC (e.g., '2026-04-13T18:00:00Z'). Feature override expires after this time.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the temporary change

directory_entry.description: Directory to scan for auto-discovery of tool permissions (NOT filesystem path blocking). Scans directory or GitHub repo for permission files and generates tool permission rules. Purpose: Auto-populate permissions.rules, not block directories. In object format (allow/deny arrays), mode is determined by which array the entry is in. In array format, mode defaults to 'allow' if not specified.

matcher.description: Tool type to apply discovered patterns to (e.g., 'Skill', 'mcp__*'). Defaults to 'Skill' if not specified.

mode.description: Permission mode for discovered patterns. Only used in array format; ignored in object format (allow/deny arrays). Defaults to 'allow'.

url.description: Local directory path or GitHub URL to scan

token_env.description: Environment variable containing GitHub token (for private repos)

remote_url_entry.description: Remote configuration URL with optional settings

url.description: Remote configuration URL (https://) or local file path

enabled.description: Whether this remote config is enabled

token_env.description: Environment variable containing auth token for remote URL
Environment variable name containing auth token

token_file.description: Path to file containing auth token (supports ~ expansion)

console.description: Console settings (editor theme, display preferences, web console)

preferred_ui.description: Preferred UI toolkit for interactive dialogs (tray-prompt, ask-prompt). auto = cascade tkinter → NiceGUI → Textual → headless. tkinter/nicegui/textual = use only that toolkit (fall to headless if unavailable). headless = no UI dialogs, ask actions use configured fallback. Env var override: AI_GUARDIAN_PREFERRED_UI

editor_theme.description: Color theme for the JSON config editor. Options: monokai, vscode_dark, dracula, github_light

web.description: Web console settings. Launch with: ai-guardian console --web

port.description: Port for web console. Default 0 (auto-assign free port).

host.description: Bind address for web console. Default 127.0.0.1 (localhost only). Do not change unless you understand the security implications.

name.description: Human-friendly instance name. Displayed in Console banner, tray menu, REST API, and MCP. Defaults to hostname if not set.

menu_tags.description: Tags identifying this daemon for tray plugin filtering. Plugins with tags only appear on daemons with at least one matching menu_tags entry.

daemon.description: Background daemon configuration. The daemon auto-starts on any CLI command and falls back to direct processing if it cannot start.

idle_timeout_minutes.description: Minutes of inactivity before daemon auto-stops. 0 (default) disables auto-stop.

client_timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for client to wait for daemon response before falling back to direct mode.

rest_port.description: REST API port for tray-to-daemon communication. Default 63152. Set to 0 for OS-assigned port. Container daemons should use a fixed port for discovery.

rest_host.description: REST API bind address. Default 127.0.0.1 (localhost only). Set to 0.0.0.0 for container daemons that need external access.

tray.description: System tray icon settings. Disable on headless servers without a display.

enabled.description: Show system tray icon when daemon is running. Set to false on headless servers.

discovery_interval_seconds.description: How often to poll for daemon discovery (seconds).

auto_install.description: Auto-install tray shortcut, autostart, and start tray on first CLI invocation. Set false to disable. Skipped on headless servers and CI/CD environments.

discover_kubernetes.description: Enable Kubernetes pod daemon discovery. Requires kubectl and appropriate cluster access.

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace to search for daemon pods.

label_selector.description: Label selector for daemon pods. User label is added automatically.

on_scan_error.description: Global behavior when a scanner encounters an error (exception, unavailable, network issue). 'allow' (default): log warning, allow operation to proceed (fail-open). 'block': block the operation if any scanner fails (fail-closed, for strict compliance). NEW in v1.7.0.

security_instructions.description: Security instruction injection into AI context (NEW in v1.7.0, Issue #580). When enabled, security rules are injected via systemMessage on every UserPromptSubmit hook response. These rules instruct the AI to never bypass, disable, or work around security protections. Default: enabled.

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

image_scanning.description: OCR-based image scanning for secrets and PII (NEW in v1.10.0, Issue #720). Enabled by default. Scans image files during PreToolUse (file reads) and image attachments during UserPromptSubmit. PostToolUse is excluded (AI already extracted text). Requires rapidocr-onnxruntime (included as dependency).

oneOf.description: Simple boolean format for permanent enable/disable

action.description: Action when secrets/PII found in image: 'block' prevents AI from seeing the image, 'warn' allows with warning, 'log-only' silently logs.

scan_types.description: Types of threats to scan for in OCR-extracted text.

max_processing_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for OCR processing per image.

min_confidence.description: Minimum OCR confidence threshold. Text regions below this are ignored.

redaction_method.description: Method for redacting sensitive regions in images.

qr_scanning.description: Scan QR codes in images for embedded secrets. Requires pyzbar.

face_detection.description: Detect faces in images (biometric PII). Requires opencv-python-headless.

ignore_files.description: File patterns to skip for image scanning (e.g., '*.ico', 'favicon.*').

ignore_tools.description: Tool names to skip for image scanning.

max_image_size_mb.description: Maximum image file size in MB. Larger images are skipped.

mcp_server.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) security advisor server settings. The MCP server exposes read-only security tools that AI agents can use proactively.

proactive_level.description: How aggressively the AI should use proactive security checks. 'low' (default): only check when user asks or after a block. 'medium': check unfamiliar paths and suspicious commands. 'high': check every file access and command. Higher levels add latency and token usage.

support.description: Support bundle export settings. Allows AI agents to prepare sanitized diagnostic bundles for troubleshooting, with user approval before sending.

export_destination.description: Destination for support bundles. Local path (e.g., '~/support-bundles'), S3 URI (e.g., 's3://bucket/prefix/'), GCS URI (e.g., 'gs://bucket-name/'), or email (e.g., 'mailto:support@company.com'). Default: XDG state directory (~/.local/state/ai-guardian/support-bundles/). Preconfigured by admin — agent cannot override.

method.description: Auth method: 'env' reads token from environment variable, 'none' for unauthenticated.

token_env.description: Environment variable name containing the auth token (when method='env').

email.description: SMTP email settings for mailto: destinations. Uses Python stdlib only (smtplib, email.mime, zipfile).

smtp_host.description: SMTP server hostname. Leave empty to use system mailto: fallback (opens default mail client).

smtp_port.description: SMTP server port. 587 for STARTTLS (default), 465 for implicit SSL, 25 for plain.

smtp_tls.description: Enable STARTTLS for port 587. Ignored for port 465 (uses implicit SSL).

from.description: Sender email address. Defaults to ai-guardian@hostname.

subject_prefix.description: Subject line prefix for support bundle emails.

method.description: Auth method: 'none' for corporate relays (no credentials), 'env' reads from environment variables (recommended), 'inline' stores credentials in config (doctor warns).

username_env.description: Environment variable for SMTP username (when method='env').

password_env.description: Environment variable for SMTP password (when method='env').

username.description: SMTP username (when method='inline'). Not recommended — doctor will warn.

password.description: SMTP password (when method='inline'). Not recommended — doctor will warn.

bundle_ttl_minutes.description: Minutes before a prepared bundle auto-expires. Prevents stale bundles from being sent.

oneOf.description: List of field names in this section that project-level configs cannot override.
Lower-level configs can tighten settings but not loosen them.

immutable_fields.description: Controls what project-level ai-guardian.json configs can override. Array lists locked fields. 'tighten-only' allows overrides that make settings stricter but blocks loosening.
